description: The Companies and Intellectual Property Commission, South Africa"s official body for company registration and intellectual property.
Digital Product Passport, a digital record containing information about a product"s sustainability, circularity, and other attributes.
The primary legislation governing the incorporation, registration, and management of companies in South Africa.
A trusted, immutable reference point that establishes the legal existence and authenticity of an entity in the digital realm.
The official company registration certificate issued by the CIPC, confirming the legal existence and registration details of an entity.
South Africa"s national identity card, used for biometric verification to confirm the identity of individuals.

The Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, which provides for the facilitation and regulation of electronic communications and transactions in South Africa, including principles of non-repudiation.
Broad-Based Black Economic Empowerment, a South African government policy to address historical inequalities.
The Protection of Personal Information Act, South Africa"s comprehensive data protection legislation.
Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation, an EU regulation aimed at making products more sustainable throughout their lifecycle.
